import os
from datasets import load_dataset
from transformers import AutoTokenizer

# Load dataset
ds = load_dataset('ryanmarten/OpenThoughts-1k-sample')
train = ds['train'] if 'train' in ds else next(iter(ds.values()))

# Determine domain filter and text fields based on typical schema and README guidance
# We'll handle multiple possible schemas robustly.

# Identify domain column name
domain_col = None
for cand in ['domain', 'topic', 'category']:
    if cand in train.column_names:
        domain_col = cand
        break
if domain_col is None:
    raise RuntimeError(f'No domain/topic/category column found. Columns: {train.column_names}')

# Identify text fields to tokenize. Prefer a single field like 'text' or 'content'.
text_fields_order = ['text', 'content', 'passage', 'document', 'prompt', 'question', 'input']
text_field = None
for cand in text_fields_order:
    if cand in train.column_names:
        text_field = cand
        break

# If chat/message-style data, use messages concatenation as per README norms.
use_messages = False
if 'messages' in train.column_names:
    use_messages = True

# Initialize tokenizer
tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained('Qwen/Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct')

# Filter to science domain
science_rows = [ex for ex in train if str(ex[domain_col]).strip().lower() == 'science']

# Function to build text for tokenization
def build_text(example):
    if use_messages and isinstance(example.get('messages'), list):
        # Concatenate messages as role: content lines
        parts = []
        for m in example['messages']:
            role = m.get('role', '')
            content = m.get('content', '')
            # Some datasets store content as list of dicts; flatten if necessary
            if isinstance(content, list):
                content = '\n'.join(
                    [c.get('text', '') if isinstance(c, dict) else str(c) for c in content]
                )
            parts.append(f"{role}: {content}")
        return '\n'.join(parts)
    elif text_field is not None:
        return example[text_field]
    else:
        # Fallback: concatenate all string fields
        parts = []
        for k, v in example.items():
            if isinstance(v, str):
                parts.append(v)
        return '\n'.join(parts)

# Compute total token count
total_tokens = 0
for ex in science_rows:
    text = build_text(ex)
    if not isinstance(text, str):
        text = str(text)
    # Use tokenizer to encode; do not add special tokens to count raw content
    ids = tokenizer(text, add_special_tokens=False)['input_ids']
    total_tokens += len(ids)

# Write result to /app/answer.txt as integer without spaces/commas
out_path = '/app/answer.txt'
with open(out_path, 'w') as f:
    f.write(str(total_tokens))
print('Total tokens (science):', total_tokens)
print('Wrote to', out_path)

Current Terminal Screen:
Using a curated mix of the datasets above, we generate reasoning traces from DeepSeek-R1 and verify correctness to construct the final dataset.

![diagram](diagram.png)

The full code for the data generation pipeline is publicly available [in our github repo](https://github.com/open-thoughts/open-thoughts).

> [!NOTE] > We have released a paper for OpenThoughts! See our paper [here](https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.04178). # Open-Thoughts-1k-sample ## Dataset Description - **Homepage:** https://www.open-thoughts.ai/ - **Repository:** https://github.com/open-thoughts/open-thoughts - **Point of Contact:** [Open Thoughts Team](contact@open-thoughts.ai) This is a 1k sample of the [OpenThoughts-114k dataset](https://huggingface.co/datasets/open-thoughts/OpenThoughts-114k). Open synthetic reasoning dataset with high-quality examples covering math, science, code, and puzzles! Inspect the content with rich formatting with [Curator Viewer](https://curator.bespokelabs.ai/datasets/1389c194254c4ead96daaf145505c3d1). ### Available Subsets **default** subset containing ready-to-train data used to finetune the [OpenThinker-7B](https://huggingface.co/open-thoughts/OpenThinker-7B) and [OpenThinker-32 B](https://huggingface.co/open-thoughts/OpenThinker-32B) models: ``` ds = load_dataset("ryanmarten/OpenThoughts-1k-sample", split="train") ``` **metadata** subset containing extra columns used in dataset construction: - `problem` - `ground_truth_solution` - `deepseek_reasoning` - `deepseek_solution` - `domain` - `source` - `test_cases` (code only) - `starter_code`(code only) ``` ds = load_dataset("ryanmarten/OpenThoughts-1k-sample", "metadata", split="train") ``` # OpenThinker Models The numbers reported in the tables below are evaluated with our open-source tool [Evalchemy](https://github.com/mlfoundations/Evalchemy). | | AIME24 | MATH500 | GPQA-Diamond | LCBv2 Easy | LCBv2 Medium | LCBv2 Hard | LCBv2 All | | --------------------------- | -------- | ------- | ------------ | ----------- | ------------- | ----------- | ---------- | | [OpenThinker-32B](https://huggingface.co/open-thoughts/OpenThinker-32B) | 66 | 90.6 | 61.6 | 95.1 | 70.9 | 26.8 | 68.9 | | [OpenThinker-7B](https://huggingface.co/open-thoughts/OpenThinker-7B) | 31.3 | 83.0 | 42.4 | 75.3 | 28.6 | 6.5 | 39.9 | | Bespoke-Stratos-7B | 22.7 | 79.6 | 38.9 | 71.4 | 25.2 | 0.8 | 35.8 | | DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B | 60 | 88.2 | 46.9 | 79.7 | 45.1 | 14.6 | 50.1 | | gpt-4o-0513 | 8.7 | 75.8 | 46.5 | 87.4 | 42.7 | 8.9 | 50.5 | | o1-mini | 64 | 85.6 | 60 | 92.8 | 74.7 | 39.8 | 72.8 | We are fully open-source. Our [model weights](https://huggingface.co/open-thoughts), [datasets](https://huggingface.co/open-thoughts), [data generation code](ht tps://github.com/open-thoughts/open-thoughts), [evaluation code](https://github.com/mlfoundations/Evalchemy), and [training code](https://github.com/hiyouga/LLa MA-Factory) are all publicly available. | | Open Weights | Open Data | Open Code | |--|--------------|-----------| --------- | |OpenThinker-32B|✅|[✅](https://huggingface.co/datasets/open-thoughts/OpenThoughts-114k)|[✅](https://github.com/open-thoughts/open-thoughts) | |OpenThinker-7B|✅|[✅](https://huggingface.co/datasets/open-thoughts/OpenThoughts-114k)|[✅](https://github.com/open-thoughts/open-thoughts) | |Bespoke-Stratos-7B|✅|[✅](https://huggingface.co/datasets/bespokelabs/Bespoke-Stratos-17k)|[✅](https://github.com/bespokelabsai/curator/tree/main/examples/be spoke-stratos-dat [... output limited to 10000 bytes; 70 interior bytes omitted ...] domain_col = cand break if domain_col is None: raise RuntimeError('Domain column not found: ' + str(train.column_names)) # Identify deepseek-related content field # Prefer reasoning/trace/thinking; else messages; else text/content content_field = None for cand in ['reasoning','trace','thinking']: if cand in train.column_names: content_field = cand break use_messages = False if content_field is None and 'messages' in train.column_names: use_messages = True elif content_field is None: for cand in ['text','content','prompt','question','input']: if cand in train.column_names: content_field = cand break # Initialize tokenizer tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained('Qwen/Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct') # Filter science domain science_rows = [ex for ex in train if str(ex[domain_col]).strip().lower() == 'science'] # Build text per example def build_text(ex): if content_field is not None: return ex.get(content_field, '') if use_messages: msgs = ex.get('messages', []) # Concatenate role: content parts = [] for m in msgs: role = m.get('role','') content = m.get('content','') if isinstance(content, list): content = '\n'.join( [c.get('text','') if isinstance(c, dict) else str(c) for c in content] ) parts.append(f"{role}: {content}") return '\n'.join(parts) # Fallback: concatenate all string fields parts = [] for k,v in ex.items(): if isinstance(v, str): parts.append(v) return '\n'.join(parts) # Token count total = 0 for ex in science_rows: txt = build_text(ex) ids = tokenizer(txt, add_special_tokens=False)['input_ids'] total += len(ids) with open('/app/answer.txt','w') as f: f.write(str(total)) print('Total science tokens:', total) PY Columns: ['system', 'conversations'] Example 0 keys: ['system', 'conversations'] Example 1 keys: ['system', 'conversations'] Example 2 keys: ['system', 'conversations'] root@rack-ge107-ya-20250517193240:/app# python3 - << 'PY' > from datasets import load_dataset > from transformers import AutoTokenizer > > ds = load_dataset('ryanmarten/OpenThoughts-1k-sample') > train = ds['train'] if 'train' in ds else next(iter(ds.values())) > > # Identify domain column > domain_col = None > for cand in ['domain','topic','category']: > if cand in train.column_names: > domain_col = cand > break > if domain_col is None: > raise RuntimeError('Domain column not found: ' + str(train.column_names)) > > # {
  "analysis": "We inspected the dataset README and confirmed there are two subsets: default (system, conversations) and metadata (problem, deepseek_reasoning, deepseek_solution, ground_truth_solution, domain, source, test_cases, starter_code). The task is to count DeepSeek tokens in the science domain using Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct tokenizer. The unique domain values in the metadata split are math, code, biology, puzzle, chemistry, physics. There is no literal 'science' value; science is represented by biology, chemistry, and physics. Our previous filter using datasets.filter returned 0 examples, likely due to a lambda or formatting issue. We should iterate through the dataset directly and check membership in {'biology','chemistry','physics'}, tokenize deepseek_reasoning with add_special_tokens=False, sum, and write to /app/answer.txt.",
  "plan": "1) Load the metadata split of the dataset. 2) Initialize the Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct tokenizer. 3) Iterate over all examples, select those with domain in biology, chemistry, or physics, and sum token counts of deepseek_reasoning with add_special_tokens=False. 4) Write the integer total to /app/answer.txt and display it.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "python3 - << 'PY'
from datasets import load_dataset
from transformers import AutoTokenizer

# Load metadata split
ds = load_dataset('ryanmarten/OpenThoughts-1k-sample', 'metadata', split='train')

science_labels = {'biology','chemistry','physics'}

# Initialize tokenizer
tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained('Qwen/Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct')

# Sum tokens for science-related domains
science_count = 0
total_tokens = 0
for ex in ds:
    dom = str(ex.get('domain','')).strip().lower()
    if dom in science_labels:
        science_count += 1
        text = ex.get('deepseek_reasoning', '')
        if not isinstance(text, str):
            text = '' if text is None else str(text)
        ids = tokenizer(text, add_special_tokens=False)['input_ids']
        total_tokens += len(ids)

with open('/app/answer.txt', 'w') as f:
    f.write(str(total_tokens))
print('Science examples:', science_count)
print('Total deepseek tokens in science domain:', total_tokens)
